# config.template chroma database leaf: make declarative, relocate UNIT_TEST_MODE override to test env

## Context

`ai/config.template.mjs` `engines.chroma.database` was one of the four inline-`process.env` leaf defaults baselined by the SSOT lint (#12474, on dev):

```js
database: leaf(process.env.UNIT_TEST_MODE === 'true' ? CHROMA_TEST_DATABASE : CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE, 'NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE', 'string')
```

The **clean, fail-closed static case** of the #12451 declarative-reshape half — the lint half landed (#12474); this leaf is the first reshape burndown.

## Shipped Contract (3-leaf toggle) — Contract Ledger

PR #12481 shipped the **3-leaf toggle** design steered by @tobiu (SSOT + KISS + safe-by-construction), NOT the originally-planned npm-script env relocation (see **Superseded** below). Backfilled per @neo-gpt's #12481 review.

| Surface | Type | Contract |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `engines.chroma.database` | config leaf | Prod DB name. Literal default `'default_database'`; env override `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE`. No inline `process.env`. |
| `engines.chroma.databaseTest` | config leaf | Test DB name. Literal default `'neo-unit-test'`; env override `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE_TEST`. |
| `engines.chroma.useTestDatabase` | config leaf | Selection toggle. Default `false`; env override `UNIT_TEST_MODE` (boolean, env-arg form — lint-clean). |
| `ChromaManager.resolveChromaClientConfig()` | consumer | Reads the resolved toggle; returns `databaseTest` when on, else `database`. Pure config reads, no inline `process.env`. |
| fail-closed guard (`ChromaManager`) | invariant | Refuses when the resolved DB === `database` while the toggle is on (refuses prod-DB-under-test → loud, never silent bleed). |

**Safe-by-construction:** both DB names are **config values** (not an npm-script-only env). Bare `npx playwright` (without `npm run test-unit`) still resolves the toggle from `UNIT_TEST_MODE` and points at `databaseTest` — it cannot bleed unit collections into production. *(Caveat: requires the gitignored `ai/config.mjs` overlay to be current — see PR #12481 Rollout Note.)*

## ~~The Fix~~ — SUPERSEDED

> ⚠️ **Superseded by the 3-leaf toggle above** (per @tobiu's design steer + @neo-gpt's #12481 review). The original plan — make the leaf declarative + relocate the test override to the `test-unit` npm-script shell env (`NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE=neo-unit-test`) — was **rejected**: moving the test DB *name* into an npm-script-only env meant bare `npx playwright` lost it → prod-bleed risk (`"oh, I forgot to use npm run test-unit"`). The shipped design keeps both names as config values + a toggle. Original text retained for provenance:

> ~~Make the leaf declarative — `leaf(CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE, 'NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE', 'string')` — and relocate the test override to the `test-unit` npm script shell env (`NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE=neo-unit-test`). Drop the now-reshaped `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE` row from the lint `BASELINE`.~~

## Acceptance Criteria (shipped)

- [x] `engines.chroma.database` leaf is declarative (no inline `process.env`); env override via the `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE` arg.
- [x] Test DB selection is **safe-by-construction**: `databaseTest` is a config leaf + the `useTestDatabase` toggle (NOT an npm-script-only env — **supersedes** the original `test-unit`-env AC).
- [x] The `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE` row is removed from the SSOT-lint `BASELINE` (lint green: 3 baselined, no new violation, no stale row).
- [x] Tenant/store isolation unaffected (the `ChromaManager` fail-closed guard is the by-construction backstop).

## Scope

Chroma database leaf only. The graph (`NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH`) static case + the dynamic collection-name cases remain on #12451 (their own follow-ups — distinct risk/complexity).

Refs #12451 (reshape umbrella). Part of Epic #12456 (AiConfig reactive Provider SSOT cleanup).

Origin Session ID: 3ecb40bf-bfef-40b1-8693-a8aae5afa1b7
Authored by Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code), /lead-role.
Contract Ledger + supersede annotation backfilled per @neo-gpt #12481 review (2026-06-04).

- 2026-06-04T08:41:21Z @neo-claude-opus referenced in commit `f1c73a7` - "refactor(ai): config.template chroma leaf uses inline literal, not an imported constant (#12480)

Operator correction: config.template IS the SSOT — it must define its values inline, never import them. The prior reshape swapped the inline UNIT_TEST_MODE branch for an imported CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE constant — still 'config depending on an external var', the same antipattern. Use the literal 'default_database'; drop the chromaTestIsolation import entirely (config.template now imports zero config values).

chromaTestIsolation.mjs keeps its CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE constant as an INDEPENDENT defense-in-depth guard invariant — the ChromaManager fail-closed guard must not trust config (that is the point of the guard), so this is intentional, not a config-value duplication. 13/13 specs pass (config.template resolution + ChromaManager isolation + the fail-closed guard); lint green at 3 baselined.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Claude Opus <neo-claude-opus@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-06-04T09:19:53Z @neo-claude-opus referenced in commit `d0c46e3` - "refactor(ai): chroma DB selection via 3 declarative config leaves + toggle (#12480)

Per @tobiu's design steer: config.template is the SSOT and the SSOT lint ENABLES env-driven values via the env-arg form — so the KISS shape is three declarative leaves: database ('default_database' literal), databaseTest ('neo-unit-test' literal), useTestDatabase (leaf(false,'UNIT_TEST_MODE','boolean') — env-driven toggle). config.template imports zero config values.

ChromaManager (consumer) reads the resolved toggle and selects databaseTest when on, else database — pure config reads, no inline process.env. Because BOTH names are config values (not an npm-script-only env var), 'npx playwright' without 'npm run test-unit' still toggles to the test DB and CANNOT bleed unit collections into production by construction — fixing the bleed risk in the prior env-var cut. The fail-closed guard now reads config (refuse if resolved === database while the toggle is on).

SSOT lint green at 3 baselined (the toggle leaf is declarative, not inline process.env). Tests updated + self-validating: the toggle resolves true under UNIT_TEST_MODE -> the resolver returns databaseTest, never default_database. 53 specs pass (ChromaManager + config.template + lint + chromaTestIsolation + purge + canonical-guard).

Co-Authored-By: Neo Claude Opus <neo-claude-opus@neomjs.com>"
### @neo-claude-opus - 2026-06-04T09:21:51Z

Design evolved to the **3-leaf toggle** (`database` + `databaseTest` + `useTestDatabase`) per @tobiu's SSOT+KISS steer — supersedes this ticket's original env-var-relocation mechanism (which bled on bare `npx`). The ACs (declarative chroma config, no inline process.env, test-isolation by construction) are met; the test DB is now a config value selected via the toggle, so no env var the runner must remember to set. PR #12481 head `d0c46e318`.

- 2026-06-04T10:47:57Z @tobiu referenced in commit `3104248` - "refactor(ai): make config.template chroma database leaf declarative (#12480) (#12481)

* refactor(ai): make config.template chroma database leaf declarative (#12480)

engines.chroma.database branched inline on process.env.UNIT_TEST_MODE (CHROMA_TEST_DATABASE : CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE) -- imperative env-resolution in the declarative config SSOT (the antipattern the #12474 lint now bans). Make it declarative: leaf(CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE, 'NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE', 'string'); the unit suite sets NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE=neo-unit-test in the test-unit npm script (the reliable worker-reaching channel). Drop the now-unused CHROMA_TEST_DATABASE import.

Fail-closed: ChromaManager refuses the production database under UNIT_TEST_MODE, so a missed override fails loud -- never silent prod pollution. Verified: 21 ChromaManager specs pass (isolation + the fail-closed guard), incl. 'targets the isolated test database' + 'fails closed ... refuses production under UNIT_TEST_MODE'.

Drop the NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE row from the SSOT-lint BASELINE (lint: 3 baselined, green). The graph + dynamic-collection-name reshapes remain on #12451 (distinct risk).

Co-Authored-By: Neo Claude Opus <neo-claude-opus@neomjs.com>

Use the literal 'default_database'; drop the chromaTestIsolation import entirely (config.template now imports zero config values).

chromaTestIsolation.mjs keeps its CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE constant as an INDEPENDENT defense-in-depth guard invariant — the ChromaManager fail-closed guard must not trust config (that is the point of the guard), so this is intentional, not a config-value duplication. 13/13 specs pass (config.template resolution + ChromaManager isolation + the fail-closed guard); lint green at 3 baselined.

ChromaManager (consumer) reads the resolved toggle and selects databaseTest when on, else database — pure config reads, no inline process.env.
